task: Classify customer product reviews as positive or negative to help an e-commerce business detect product problems early. Train a binary text classifier and evaluate it. Business goal: automatically flag negative reviews so the team can prioritize complaints, fix product listings, and improve customer satisfaction faster than manual review.
llm_decisions: Full 7-agent pipeline planned for end-to-end NLP binary classification task
Logistic Regression selected over Random Forest (CV F1: 0.9296 vs 0.9225)
TF-IDF bigrams (5000 features) + numeric FeatureUnion chosen as feature strategy
class_weight='balanced' applied to handle 76/24 class imbalance
C tuned from 1.0 to 5.0 via RandomizedSearchCV, improving CV F1 by ~0.7 points
Model declared ready: F1=0.97, AUC=0.99, no catastrophic failure mode
key_findings: 20,000 reviews, zero missing values, zero duplicates — clean dataset
76.17% positive / 23.83% negative class imbalance handled with class_weight='balanced'
Negative class recall of 96.0% — model catches nearly all real complaints
Negative class precision of 84.8% — ~15% false alarm rate on flagged negatives
ROC-AUC of 98.74% enables flexible threshold tuning without retraining
Outliers in exclamation_count (705) and uppercase_ratio (665) retained as real signal
Evaluation metrics may be slightly optimistic due to overlap between clean.csv and training data
issues: Evaluation done on clean.csv which likely overlaps training data — metrics may be optimistic; trainer's held-out test metrics (F1=0.9415, AUC=0.9631) are more reliable
Negative class precision is 84.8% — ~15% of flagged negatives are false alarms
Outliers in exclamation_count and uppercase_ratio (informational only, retained)
